dosing_enrichment.titration_note: Start Ipamorelin 200 mcg + CJC-1295 100 mcg 2x/day (AM + bedtime) for weeks 1-4. If well tolerated and IGF-1 response confirmed at week 4, add midday dose (3x/day) for weeks 5-12. Monitor IGF-1 monthly.
dosing_enrichment.cycling_recommendation: 12-week active cycle followed by 4-6 week washout. GH axis desensitization prevents with cycling; HPTA recovery confirmed within 4-6 weeks off protocol per IGF-1 normalization data.
dosing_enrichment.maintenance_dose: Ipamorelin: 300 mcg subcutaneous 3x/day (morning, pre-workout, bedtime). CJC-1295 (no DAC): 100-200 mcg subcutaneous 3x/day co-administered with Ipamorelin.
dosing_enrichment.timing_optimization: Administer in fasted state (90 min post-meal) for optimal GH pulse. Bedtime dose is most physiologically relevant - mimics natural nocturnal GH surge. Avoid administration after large carbohydrate meals as insulin blunts GH release.

protocol_author_notes: Official curated protocol for general clinical use.

qualitative: Increased lean muscle mass and reduced visceral fat
Improved sleep quality and recovery speed
Enhanced tissue repair and skin collagen production
Improved insulin sensitivity and metabolic rate
Improved recovery capacity and sleep quality
Support for lean mass preservation and body composition
Potential increase in IGF-1 within monitored physiological target range
Reduced subjective fatigue in GH-axis low-response phenotypes

quantitative_ranges.week_8: IGF-1 near peak response; body composition shift visible; skin texture improvement reported in 60-70% of patients
quantitative_ranges.week_4: IGF-1 levels increase 15-25%; subjective sleep quality improvement in 70% of patients; recovery speed improvement
quantitative_ranges.week_12: IGF-1 increase 30-50% from baseline; lean mass gain 2-4 kg; fat mass reduction 3-5%; sleep quality (PSQI) improvement 20-35%

expected_outcomes.responder_rate_pct: 75-80% for IGF-1 response; 70% for body composition improvement; 80% for sleep quality improvement

overview_summary: Advanced protocol for hormonal optimization using growth hormone secretagogues.

special_notes: Use conservative ramping if sensitivity profile is high.
Reassess tolerance and response before escalation.

monitoringSchedule.label: End-of-Protocol Evaluation
tests: Full hormone panel: IGF-1, GH, cortisol, thyroid (TSH, fT4)
monitoringSchedule.notes: Full outcome assessment and decision on continuation or cycling.

side_effects.management: Rotate sites; use proper aseptic technique.
Reduce dose; monitor blood pressure; salt restriction.
Dose with food; split doses; titrate slowly.
Monitor fasting glucose weekly; consult if persistent.
Reduce dose; psychological support if needed.

escalation_criteria: Severe or worsening edema — STOP, cardiovascular evaluation
Fasting glucose > 7.0 mmol/L (126 mg/dL) on two occasions — Endocrinology referral
Hypertensive urgency (BP > 180/110) — STOP, immediate medical attention
riskManagement.storage_handling: Store peptides at 2–8°C (refrigerated). Reconstituted vials: stable for 28 days refrigerated. Do not freeze reconstituted product. Protect from light.
patient_education: Demonstrate subcutaneous injection technique before starting.
Provide written dosing schedule and injection site rotation map.
Instruct patient to report any unusual symptoms within 24h.
Provide emergency contact number for out-of-hours concerns.

clinical_disclaimer: For use by qualified healthcare professionals only. Not a diagnosis, prescription, or substitute for individualized medical judgment, informed consent, and local regulatory compliance.

evidence_limitations: Several peptide uses in this library remain off-label, investigational, exploratory, or based on translational evidence.
Protocol data should not be interpreted as individualized medical advice or a substitute for prescribing clinician judgment.
Regulatory status varies by country and must be checked locally before clinical use or commercial distribution.
clinical_evidence_update_2026.update_scope: missing clinical/regulatory/safety information added while preserving canonical antigravity_v2 data model
latest_clinical_notes: GH secretagogue combinations remain research/off-label in most jurisdictions.
Baseline IGF-1, fasting glucose/HbA1c, malignancy history, sleep apnea risk, edema, and carpal tunnel symptoms should be explicitly monitored.
Avoid implying equivalence with FDA-approved GH replacement.
clinical_evidence_update_2026.clinical_use_guardrail: Use under qualified medical supervision where legally permitted; obtain informed consent for off-label or investigational use.
clinical_evidence_update_2026.regulatory_status: CJC-1295, ipamorelin, kisspeptin, gonadorelin and related endocrine peptide protocols are not general FDA/EMA-approved longevity protocols. Use should remain physician-supervised with endocrine and cancer-screening precautions.
clinical_evidence_update_2026.evidence_update: Maintain IGF-1/glucose/pituitary/endocrine monitoring and avoid use in active malignancy or uncontrolled diabetes.
clinical_evidence_update_2026.clinical_use_note: This protocol is informational and designed for clinician-supervised review. It is not a standalone prescription, diagnosis, or patient-specific treatment plan.

metadata.longDescription: CJC-1295 (a synthetic analogue of growth hormone-releasing hormone, GHRH 1-29, with Drug Affinity Complex modification) extends the half-life of endogenous GHRH from minutes to days by binding covalently to serum albumin, producing sustained elevation of GH and IGF-1 without desensitisation of the GHRH receptor (Teichman SL et al., J Clin Endocrinol Metab 2006). Ipamorelin (Aib-His-D-2Nal-D-Phe-Lys-NH₂) is a selective GH secretagogue receptor (GHSR) agonist (ghrelin mimetic) that stimulates pulsatile GH release with high receptor selectivity, causing minimal elevation of cortisol, prolactin, or ACTH compared to earlier secretagogues (Raun K et al., Eur J Endocrinol 1998). The combination produces synergistic GH release: CJC-1295 raises the baseline amplitude of GH secretion while Ipamorelin drives discrete pulsatile peaks, together approximating the physiological GH secretion pattern of younger adults. Tesamorelin is added during the activation phase for its validated visceral fat reduction in GH-deficient patients (Falutz J et al., NEJM 2007).
metadata.target_patient_profile: Adults 30-65 with low-normal IGF-1, age-related somatopause symptoms, poor recovery, reduced lean mass, or disrupted sleep architecture. Ideal for patients seeking anti-aging hormonal support without exogenous GH administration. Particularly indicated in individuals with GH deficiency features but normal pituitary function.
metadata.short_description: A 12-week GH axis optimization protocol using Ipamorelin and CJC-1295 to stimulate physiological GH pulses, improve body composition, sleep quality, and tissue recovery.
metadata.source_quality_note: Clinical content consolidated from uploaded protocol files and public biomedical literature references already embedded in protocol metadata; final medical use requires qualified clinician review.

references.citation: Teichman SL et al. CJC-1295 sustained GH release. J Clin Endocrinol Metab 2006;91:799-805.
Raun K et al. Ipamorelin, a new growth hormone releasing peptide. Eur J Endocrinol 1998;139:552-561.
Falutz J et al. Tesamorelin reduces visceral adiposity in HIV. NEJM 2007;357:2349-2361.

metadata.scientificName: CJC-1295 / Ipamorelin GHRH & Ghrelin-Mimetic GH Secretagogue Optimization Protocol
metadata.evidence_grade_note: GRADE B: Ipamorelin Phase I/II data (Raun K, Eur J Endocrinol 1998); CJC-1295 Phase II human PK/PD data (Teichman SL, J Clin Endocrinol Metab 2006). IGF-1 endpoints well-validated. Limited long-term RCT data for body composition endpoints.
metadata.description: 12-week GH axis optimization protocol combining CJC-1295 GHRH analogue sustained release with Ipamorelin selective ghrelin-mimetic secretagogue for pulsatile physiological GH restoration.
metadata.clinical_summary: Ipamorelin is a selective GH secretagogue that stimulates pituitary GH release without affecting cortisol, prolactin, or ACTH - providing a clean GH pulse with minimal side effects. CJC-1295 (without DAC) is a GHRH analog that amplifies and prolongs GH pulses via pituitary somatotroph stimulation. Used together, they synergistically maximize GH pulse amplitude and frequency, producing elevations in IGF-1 that drive anabolic, lipolytic, and reparative tissue effects.

pubmed_references.title: Ipamorelin, the first selective growth hormone secretagogue
Prolonged stimulation of growth hormone (GH) and insulin-like growth factor I secretion by CJC-1295
The Safety and Efficacy of Growth Hormone Secretagogues

metadata.regulatory_note: Ipamorelin and CJC-1295: Not FDA-approved for weight management, anti-aging, or body composition. FDA issued warning letters against compounding of these peptides in 2024 (Category 2 bulk substances). Available via 503A compounding pharmacies under physician supervision in jurisdictions where permitted. Not WADA-prohibited if used therapeutically.

scheduled_checkpoints.purpose: Confirm early GH-axis response and tolerability.
Assess endocrine response and cardiometabolic safety.
Determine continuation, taper, or washout plan.

profile_notes: Use clinician-supervised interpretation if pairing with other endocrine therapies.

drug_interactions: Insulin: GH counterregulatory effect may impair glucose homeostasis - monitor in diabetics
Glucocorticoids: blunt GH secretagogue response - avoid concurrent use if possible
Thyroid hormones: hypothyroidism attenuates GH response - ensure euthyroid state at baseline
SSRIs: no significant interaction at standard doses
monitoring_required: IGF-1 at baseline, week 4, week 8, week 12 (target: upper-normal range, not supraphysiologic)
Fasting glucose and HbA1c at baseline and week 12
Body composition (DEXA or InBody) at baseline and week 12
PSQI sleep score at baseline, week 4, week 12
adverse_events_serious: No serious adverse events in Phase I/II trials at standard doses
Theoretical IGF-1 excess risk at supraphysiologic doses - maintain IGF-1 within upper normal range
Contraindicated in active malignancy - IGF-1 is mitogenic; avoid in cancer history without oncology clearance
adverse_events_common: Transient water retention: 10-20% in first 4 weeks, resolves with adaptation
Mild tingling or numbness in extremities (carpal tunnel-like): 8-12%
Injection site reactions: 5-10% - mild, rotate sites
Increased hunger: 15-20% - expected from ghrelin-receptor activity of Ipamorelin
common_side_effects: Injection-site redness, itching, or mild swelling (SC injection, ~25–30% of users)
Water retention and mild peripheral edema, particularly in weeks 2–4 (~20%)
Transient tingling or paresthesia in extremities (carpal tunnel-like, ~15%)
Fatigue or somnolence 30–60 minutes after evening injection (~10%)
Mild headache during initial GH axis stimulation (~8%)
Increased appetite and food cravings, especially in evening (~15%)
safety_profile.contraindication_summary: Absolute contraindications: active malignancy or history of hormone-sensitive cancer (GH axis stimulation may promote tumor proliferation); pituitary adenoma or active pituitary pathology; uncontrolled diabetes mellitus (GH raises insulin resistance — monitor fasting glucose and HbA1c); untreated severe obstructive sleep apnea (GH pulses worsen OSA); pregnancy or breastfeeding. Relative contraindications: pre-existing carpal tunnel syndrome (fluid retention may exacerbate); active thyroid dysfunction (correct before initiating); concomitant exogenous GH or IGF-1 therapy. Monitor IGF-1 levels at baseline, week 6, and week 12. Physician supervision mandatory throughout.
